all.en: Every Sunday evening, the Lee family had a video call with relatives in Vancouver.
all.zh: 每個星期日晚上，李家都會和溫哥華的親戚視訊通話。

all.en: Grandpa, who was eighty years old, struggled to figure out the camera angle.
all.zh: 八十歲的爺爺努力想搞清楚鏡頭的角度。

all.en: "I can only see your forehead, Dad!" Aunt Linda shouted from the screen.
all.zh: 「爸，我只看得到你的額頭！」琳達阿姨在螢幕另一頭大喊。

all.en: Grandpa laughed and tilted the tablet up until his whole face appeared.
all.zh: 爺爺笑了，把平板往上斜，直到整張臉都出現在畫面裡。

all.en: Cousin Ryan, who was now twelve, showed off the snow falling outside his window.
all.zh: 已經十二歲的表弟雷恩展示著窗外飄下的雪。

all.en: His Taiwanese cousins gasped because they had never seen real snow before.
all.zh: 他在台灣的表兄弟姊妹都驚呼起來，因為他們從來沒看過真正的雪。

all.en: Aunt Linda announced that she was finally pregnant after years of trying.
all.zh: 琳達阿姨宣布在嘗試多年後，她終於懷孕了。

all.en: Everyone in Taiwan cheered, and Grandma started crying tears of happiness.
all.zh: 台灣這邊的所有人都歡呼起來，奶奶開心得流下眼淚。

all.en: They talked about plans to visit each other during the summer holidays.
all.zh: 他們聊著暑假時要互相拜訪的計畫。

all.en: Grandpa promised to bring his famous beef jerky when they flew to Canada.
all.zh: 爺爺答應飛去加拿大時要帶上他出名的牛肉乾。

all.en: The call lasted nearly two hours and felt much shorter than that.
all.zh: 視訊持續了快兩個小時，但感覺比實際時間短得多。

all.en: When they finally said goodbye, the distance between them felt much smaller.
all.zh: 當他們終於說再見時，彼此之間的距離感覺縮短了許多。
